Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First looking at Group A All entries have LocalResident as FALSE and CommunityGroup as None The PreliminaryScore stays the same throughout no adjustments The decisions are Approved Rejected Approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now Group B Here LocalResident is TRUE for some cases For U001 and U003 CommunityGroup is Highland Civic Darts Club and they have a ScoreAdjustment of plus 10 Both are approved U002 has LocalResident TRUE but CommunityGroup is None so no adjustment Their score remains 710 and is rejected

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: when CommunityGroup is present they get a plus 10 boost That seems like a policy where being part of a community group gives an advantage But in Group A there is no such adjustment

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: the adjusted scores in Group B allowed lower initial scores to be approved For example U003 had 695 which is lower than Group As rejected 710 but after adjustment to 705 it is approved Whereas in Group A 710 was rejected without adjustment

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That seems like a disparity So Group Bs members who are part of the community group get a boost leading to approvals even with lower initial scores

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: maybe Group B is the one with bias because they are giving extra points based on community group membership

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Wait the problem says Group A is Protected and Group B is Unprotected So perhaps the system is supposed to protect Group A from certain biases but in reality Group B is receiving adjustments

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Comparing the two groups In Group A a score of 710 is rejected In Group B a score of 710 without adjustment is also rejected But in Group B when there is a community group lower initial scores 695 get adjusted up to 705 and approved

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore the bias is in Group Bs log because they apply a community boost that is not available to Group A leading to systematically higher scores and approval rates for certain members of Group B

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This creates a systematic advantage for certain members of Group B which could be considered biased if the adjustment is not justified or is applied inconsistently
